title: hypericum humbertii
intro: Hypericum humbertii, also known as the Madagascar St. John's Wort, is a species of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort genus. It is a shrub-like plant native to the island of Madagascar and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. Herbal preparations of Hypericum humbertii can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
